admission_type: Master's degree with verification of curricular requirements and an assessment of personal preparation (prerequisites/admission test). Strong-grade candidates are exempt from the admission test. Non-EU candidates residing abroad must pass UniGeApply pre-evaluation, which includes a remote test/interview.

academic_requirements.summary: Admission requires a bachelor's degree (or equivalent) with minimum credits in Information Engineering scientific sectors, plus successful completion of a prerequisites assessment unless exempted by a high bachelor grade.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree of at least 180 CFU/ECTS, or a Laurea / Laurea Magistrale, or an equivalent qualification (including a foreign degree recognised as suitable). On UniGeApply: a bachelor in the field of engineering, computer science, or the ICT area.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: At least 36 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) acquired from any university course (first cycle, second cycle, first/second-level university masters) in the scientific sectors related to L-8 Information Engineering; and at least 45 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in the scientific sectors related to L-8 Information Engineering (Automation Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Computer Systems Engineering, Telecommunication Engineering).
academic_requirements.minimum_grade_or_cgpa: [uncertain] On UniGeApply a minimum CGPA of 75% of the maximum obtainable is listed for non-EU pre-evaluation. Separately, a bachelor grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum (or ECTS class 'A'/'B') exempts the candidate from the admission test.
academic_requirements.preparation_assessment: Applicants must successfully pass the Prerequisites assessment (admission test). If the bachelor's degree grade is at least 9/10 of the maximum, or corresponds to ECTS 'A' or 'B', the admission test is not required. Non-EU applicants additionally take a remote test/interview during UniGeApply pre-selection.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Foreign qualifications recognised as suitable are accepted; non-EU candidates residing abroad apply through UniGeApply for pre-evaluation before enrolment.

accepted_proofs: [uncertain] The admission page states 'English language, B2 level or equivalent' but does not enumerate accepted certificates/scores; UniGe accepts the standard certificates for its English-taught masters (e.g. TOEFL, Cambridge B2 First, IELTS, TOEIC, Trinity, IGCSE) and a Medium-of-Instruction (MOI) certificate.
On UniGeApply the programme lists an English certificate (or MOI) among required documents.
exemption_rules: [uncertain] An English certificate is generally not required if the language of instruction of the applicant's previous studies was English, officially certified (standard UniGe rule; not explicitly restated on this admission page).
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English B2 is part of the curricular/prerequisites verification; for non-EU applicants the certificate or MOI is uploaded during UniGeApply pre-evaluation.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Prerequisites assessment / Admission Test (plus a remote test or interview for non-EU UniGeApply candidates).
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants holding the curricular requirements; non-EU candidates also sit a remote test/interview during pre-selection.
entry_exam_or_test.format: Verification of the curricular CFU requirements followed by an Admission Test (prerequisites assessment). Non-EU applicants take a remote test/interview during UniGeApply pre-selection.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] Specific test/interview dates are set during the admission/pre-evaluation procedure and are not published as a fixed institution-wide date.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: No numerical pass score published. Exemption: bachelor grade at least 9/10 of the maximum, or ECTS class 'A'/'B', removes the Admission Test requirement.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
The course deadlines page shows generic enrolment-calendar dates rather than a programme-specific EU application date.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: First-year pre-registration runs from 9 April to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment); confirmation of enrolment from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment).
application_deadline_eu.notes: Fee instalments for A.Y. 2026/2027: second by 10 December 2026, third by 23 June 2027.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After UniGeApply eligibility, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment and then apply for the study visa, per the central UniGe international enrolment calendar.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published for Internet and Multimedia Engineering. The only fall-session early closures listed for A.Y. 2026/27 are Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology (13 Feb 2026), Maritime Science and Technology bachelor (18 Feb 2026), and Computer Engineering bachelor (8 Mar 2026).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session; the A.Y. 2026/27 fall window has closed (20 Mar 2026). UniGeApply allows 1 to 2 assessment requests per applicant. Non-EU applicants face a remote test/interview during pre-selection.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records / list of exams with credits
required_documents.notes: Used to verify the 36 CFU and 45 CFU Information Engineering subject-sector requirements.
Bachelor of at least 180 CFU/ECTS in engineering, computer science, or the ICT area.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate (B2) or Medium-of-Instruction (MOI) certificate
required_documents.notes: B2-level English proof; an MOI letter is accepted when prior studies were taught in English.
Passport is the only accepted ID for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply (photo as JPG, supporting documents as PDF up to 2 MB).

required_documents.document_name: Legalization / CIMEA Statement of Verification and Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU visa applicants with a foreign qualification to complete enrolment.

source_quotes.quote: INTERNET AND MULTIMEDIA ENGINEERING ... Degree class LM-27 (Telecommunications Engineering) ... Genova ... Language: English ... 120 CFU, 2 years

at least 36 CFU, or equivalent knowledge ... in the scientific sectors related to the L8- Information Engineering ... at least 45 CFU, or equivalent knowledge ... in the scientific sectors related to the L-8 Information Engineering: Automation Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Computer Systems Engineering, Telecommunication Engineering

If your Bachelor's degree grade is at least 9/10 of the maximum or has obtained a final grade corresponding to the 'A' or 'B' classification of the ECTS system, you will not have to take the Admission Test.
English language, B2 level or equivalent
Internet and Multimedia Engineering: minimum CGPA 75%; remote test/interview required during pre-selection; Bachelor in the field of engineering, computer science, ICT area
Fall session (visa applicants): opens 26 November 2025, closes 20 March 2026, results by 22 May 2026. Spring session (residents): opens 9 April 2026, master's close 28 August 2026.

uncertainty_notes: Teaching language confirmed: completely English (LM-27, Genoa).

The admission page states the B2 English requirement but does not enumerate accepted certificates/scores or the MOI exemption explicitly; certificate details are inferred from UniGe's standard English-master rules and the UniGeApply 'English certificate (or MOI)' note.
Two subject-sector CFU thresholds are published (36 CFU and 45 CFU, both linked to L-8 Information Engineering); they read as cumulative curricular requirements on the official page.

The 75% minimum CGPA and the remote test/interview come from the UniGeApply non-EU master's pre-evaluation summary; confirm against the live listing. The bachelor-grade exemption (9/10 or ECTS A/B) from the Admission Test is stated on the official course admission page.

The per-programme course deadlines page shows generic enrolment-calendar dates; the authoritative 2026/27 application windows used here are the institution-wide UniGeApply fall (visa) and spring (resident) sessions. No fall-session early closure is published for this programme.
